class.name: CSGTorus3D
class.description: This node allows you to create a torus for use with the CSG system. [b]Note:[/b] CSG nodes are intended to be used for level prototyping. Creating CSG nodes has a significant CPU cost compared to creating a [MeshInstance3D] with a [PrimitiveMesh]. Moving a CSG node within another CSG node also has a significant CPU cost, so it should be avoided during gameplay.

members.member: The inner radius of the torus.
The material used to render the torus.
The outer radius of the torus.
The number of edges each ring of the torus is constructed of.

Enabling this will scale the UVs to account for CSGTorus [member inner_radius] and [member outer_radius], maintaining uniform texel size. [b]Note:[/b] [member scale_uv] might have issues with non-repeating textures.
The number of slices the torus is constructed of.
If [code]true[/code] the normals of the torus are set to give a smooth effect making the torus seem rounded. If [code]false[/code] the torus will have a flat shaded look.

The number of times the texture on the CSGTorus is going to repeat horizontally. Default is 8. When [member scale_uv] is enabled, this is used to calculate the number of horizontal repetitions on the UVs of a torus. Increasing the radius will increase the repetitions, while reducing it will reduce the number of repetitions.
Offsets the UVs on the side faces of the CSGTorus.
Uniformly scales UVs to set the texel size.

The number of times the texture on the CSGTorus is going to repeat vertically. Default is 4. When [member scale_uv] is enabled, this is used to calculate the number of vertical repetitions on the UVs of a torus. Increasing the radius will increase the repetitions, while reducing it will reduce the number of repetitions.
